title: [bug] HostWindow thread affinity deadlocks main thread after fullscreen
description:
## Root cause (proven by logs 2026-04-19 23:12:30)

HostWindow's HWND is created inside `ensure_init`, which is called from `#[tauri::command] async fn player_load_url`. Async Tauri commands run on a tokio worker thread, so the HWND is thread-affine to that tokio worker.

Win32 SetWindowPos called cross-thread does SendMessage to the owner thread and waits for the owner to dispatch it. Tokio workers don't pump Win32 messages, so the call blocks indefinitely.

During normal drag-resize this is sporadically OK (the worker thread occasionally gets awakened by tokio activity that happens to service the queue), but the fullscreen path in `player_set_fullscreen`'s `run_on_main_thread` closure deadlocks reliably: the main thread goes into SetWindowPos and never returns. Evidence: `set_geometry` logs fire, but the follow-up `sync_geometry returned` log never does.

With the main thread stuck, Tauri's IPC handler stops processing. When React's cleanup invokes `player_unload`, the command queues but never reaches the backend — explaining the 'audio continues 20s, no [player:cmd] unload in log' report.

## Fix
Own the HostWindow on the main thread. `ensure_init` dispatches HostWindow creation via `app.run_on_main_thread` and a one-shot channel to wait for the result. `destroy` dispatches DestroyWindow the same way. SetWindowPos from any thread to a main-thread-owned window works cleanly because main always pumps messages.

## Acceptance
- Toggle fullscreen, click back → audio cuts immediately and `[player:cmd] unload` appears in log
- Drag-resize still works (same behavior as today)
- No 'cursor is loading spinner' regression

## Required Skills
rust, tauri, prexu, anti-fabrication
close_reason: Resolved on feature/native-player. u4c: HostWindow now owned on main thread (f5bc7bc) — fullscreen + un-fullscreen confirmed working. 31n: suspected commits 252b631/d2349ee/fee7d2e were reverted; safer re-application landed in 6f937e1 and HostWindow main-thread fix; user confirmed nav/play/pause work without unresponsive window.

title: [bug] Native player: main window unresponsive on first Player load (regression)
description:
## Symptom
After my 3 commits (252b631, d2349ee, fee7d2e), launching the app and navigating to the Player:
- Video plays (mpv renders, audio audible)
- Tauri main window does not accept focus
- Windows shows the busy/wait cursor (IDC_APPSTARTING / IDC_WAIT) over the app
- Keyboard input goes to the terminal, not the app (Esc cancels shell input)
- Only escape hatch is Ctrl+C on the terminal

Logs confirm:
- FileLoaded → player://ready fires normally
- time-pos progresses (video is decoding)
- No errors or warnings from player subsystems
- No set_fullscreen command ever fires — user can't trigger it

## Suspected causes (in order of likelihood)

1. **252b631 (destroy joins event pump)**: handle.join() blocks the calling thread. If something during the StrictMode double-mount interleaves destroy() with a cross-thread SetWindowPos from the Tauri UI thread targeting our host HWND (owned by a tokio worker), main would SendMessage-block while the worker is blocked on join(). But destroy should be a no-op on first load (Inner=None) so this shouldn't actually fire.
2. **d2349ee (WM_SIZE → async child resize)**: new WndProc logic that runs whenever WM_SIZE hits our host. The SWP_ASYNCWINDOWPOS flag should make it non-blocking, but re-entrancy or an unforeseen message path could stall something.
3. **fee7d2e (cleanup chain)**: cleanup now chains unload → fullscreen-exit. A fire-and-forget Promise chain in useEffect cleanup shouldn't block the JS thread, and the Rust side is independent.

## Next step
Revert all 3 commits, rebuild, retest. If the hang disappears, bisect to find the specific regression.

## Required Skills
rust, tauri, prexu, anti-fabrication
close_reason: Resolved on feature/native-player. u4c: HostWindow now owned on main thread (f5bc7bc) — fullscreen + un-fullscreen confirmed working. 31n: suspected commits 252b631/d2349ee/fee7d2e were reverted; safer re-application landed in 6f937e1 and HostWindow main-thread fix; user confirmed nav/play/pause work without unresponsive window.

title: [bug] Native player: mpv D3D11 swapchain doesn't resize on fullscreen
description:
## Root cause

The host HWND correctly resizes to display dimensions (3840x2160) during fullscreen — confirmed in commit 8196e41 and the beads memory. `SetWindowPos` in `HostWindow::set_geometry` succeeds. But mpv's internal D3D11 swapchain stays at the pre-fullscreen size.

In `src-tauri/src/player/host_window.rs:30-37`, the WndProc is just `DefWindowProcW`. mpv creates a child render surface inside our HWND (confirmed by earlier working fix at commit b261182, which found a child via `GetWindow(hwnd, GW_CHILD)`). When the parent HWND resizes, WM_SIZE fires on the parent, but child windows in Win32 do NOT auto-resize — the parent must explicitly move children. Our WndProc swallows WM_SIZE into DefWindowProcW with no child handling.

Previous fix b261182 added WM_SIZE → MoveWindow on GW_CHILD inside our WndProc. This was reverted in 30a4949 because "SetWindowPos on mpv's child triggered a D3D11 swapchain rebuild that blocked the main thread". The PostMessage variant (`post_resize` in older code) was also removed as dead code in 193904c.

## Fix options (in order of preference)

**Option A — non-blocking child resize via SWP_ASYNCWINDOWPOS + bRepaint=false:**
Re-add WM_SIZE handler in `wnd_proc` that resizes GW_CHILD with `MoveWindow(child, 0, 0, w, h, false /* bRepaint */)` or `SetWindowPos(..., SWP_ASYNCWINDOWPOS | SWP_NOREDRAW)`. The async flag posts the resize to mpv's thread's queue instead of sending synchronously, avoiding the main-thread block that motivated 30a4949.

**Option B — tell mpv to reconfigure:**
After sync_geometry applies a large jump (post-fullscreen), invoke an mpv command like `mpv.set_property("window-scale", 1.0)` or similar to force it to re-read window size. Lower risk of deadlock but depends on whether libmpv exposes a reconfigure trigger we can call off-thread.

**Option C — subclass + explicit swapchain size:**
Use `SetWindowSubclass` on mpv's child HWND to intercept its events. More invasive; only if A/B fail.

## Test plan
- Enter fullscreen during playback: video fills the entire screen (not just the pre-fullscreen region)
- Exit fullscreen: video returns to the window region
- Enter→exit→enter cycle: each transition correctly resizes (memory note says 2nd play has issues)
- Drag-resize the window: video follows smoothly
- No main-thread freeze during transition

## Required Skills
rust, tauri, prexu, anti-fabrication

## History
- b261182 added child resize — worked but blocked main thread
- 0e0eda8 tried SWP_ASYNCWINDOWPOS — still flaky
- 30a4949 removed child resize entirely — current state, video doesn't resize
- 193904c deleted the SWP_ASYNCWINDOWPOS constant + post_resize as dead code
close_reason: WndProc now handles WM_SIZE and resizes GW_CHILD with SWP_ASYNCWINDOWPOS + NOREDRAW flags. Async flag posts the resize to mpv's thread queue, avoiding the main-thread block that killed the earlier sync attempt. Commit d2349ee.

title: [bug] Native player: drop-order race crashes mpv on destroy
description:
## Root cause

In `src-tauri/src/player/mod.rs`, `Inner` declares fields in order: `mpv: Arc<Mpv>` first, `host: HostWindow` second. Rust drops struct fields in declaration order, so when `destroy()` runs `*guard = None;`:

1. `Arc<Mpv>` drops → refcount 2→1 (event pump thread still holds the other Arc). `mpv_terminate_destroy` is NOT called yet.
2. `HostWindow` drops → `DestroyWindow` runs on the HWND that mpv's render thread is still using.
3. Much later, the event pump's `wait_event(1.0)` returns `Event::Shutdown`, loop breaks, its Arc drops (1→0), `mpv_terminate_destroy` finally runs — but the HWND is already gone.

The window between step 2 and step 3 is the crash window: mpv's D3D11 render thread tries to present to a destroyed HWND. Verified against libmpv2 source at `~/.cargo/registry/src/index.crates.io-.../libmpv2-4.1.0/src/mpv.rs:465-468` (Drop calls `mpv_terminate_destroy`).

The existing code comment on `Inner.host` (mod.rs:56-61) is aspirational — the field order is correct in intent but drops aren't synchronous because another Arc is live.

## Also explains: audio keeps playing after navigating to dashboard

`player_unload` returns to JS as soon as `destroy()` returns — which is BEFORE mpv has actually terminated. mpv's audio output keeps feeding the device until the event pump processes Shutdown (up to ~1s due to the `wait_event(1.0)` timeout plus whatever mpv takes internally).

## Fix

Store the event pump's `JoinHandle` on `Inner`. In `destroy()`:
1. Send `stop` + `quit` to mpv.
2. Drop/replace the Arc held on the main side first (via `take()` + `drop()`).
3. `join()` the event pump thread — this returns after it drops its Arc.
4. NOW both Arcs are gone → `mpv_terminate_destroy` ran synchronously during the final Arc drop.
5. `HostWindow` drops cleanly.

After this, `player_unload` only returns to JS once mpv is fully dead + HWND destroyed. Audio stops before navigation happens.

## Acceptance
- Navigate to dashboard while playing: audio stops within one frame
- Close app while playing: no crash in logs
- Repeated play→dashboard→play cycles: no zombie mpv state

## Required Skills
rust, tauri, prexu, anti-fabrication
notes: Found during thorough app review 2026-04-19. Related to beads memory 'native-player-fullscreen-host-window-geometry-sync-confirmed'.
close_reason: Event pump JoinHandle stored in Inner; destroy() takes Inner out, sends stop+quit, joins pump, then drops. Final Arc drop runs mpv_terminate_destroy synchronously before HostWindow::drop. Commit 252b631.

title: Transparent window still visible after back-nav despite useLayoutEffect fix
description: After fb95f52, clicking back still shows Discord/desktop through the Prexu window briefly during Player→Dashboard transition. useLayoutEffect cleanup alone is insufficient; possible WebView2 compositor timing gap or a non-body transparent surface. Fix: set body background to navy imperatively in handleBack BEFORE navigate so the opaque paint is committed while Player is still mounted (container transparent still shows mpv). Belt-and-suspenders over the existing useLayoutEffect cleanup.
close_reason: Closed

title: [bug] Native player: cleanup fires fullscreen + unload in parallel
description:
## Root cause

In `src/hooks/player/useNativePlayer.ts:308-318`, the useEffect cleanup on unmount fires two Tauri invocations without ordering:

```ts
if (isFullscreenRef.current) {
  invoke("player_set_fullscreen", { fullscreen: false }).catch(() => {});
}
invoke("player_unload").catch(() => {});
```

`player_set_fullscreen` on the backend (`src-tauri/src/player/commands.rs:159`) takes ~350ms (it sleeps to wait for the Win11 transition animation). `player_unload` calls `destroy()` which sends `stop` + `quit` to mpv and drops Inner.

These two commands race. The unload runs immediately after — so mpv is destroyed while the fullscreen command is still mid-transition. Then at t+350ms the fullscreen closure runs:

```rust
st.sync_geometry(x, y, w, h);  // calls inner.host.set_geometry on a dropped inner → no-op
win_for_sync.set_focus();
```

The `last_geometry` mutex is reset to None for an Inner that no longer exists. `sync_geometry` then can't find Inner via `try_lock + as_ref` and silently no-ops. That part is benign but the overall sequence means the main window may finish the fullscreen exit AFTER the dashboard has already rendered, causing the dashboard to briefly appear fullscreen.

## Additional concern: errors are silently swallowed

Both `.catch(() => {})` calls swallow all backend errors without logging. If `player_unload` fails (e.g. lock poisoned), audio keeps playing and nothing is logged.

## Fix

- In the cleanup, don't await (can't in useEffect), but chain: invoke fullscreen → on resolve OR timeout, invoke unload. Or simpler: since destroy() is about to tear down mpv, skip the mpv-side fullscreen call entirely and just toggle the Tauri main window fullscreen off via a dedicated command that does NOT touch mpv state.
- Replace `.catch(() => {})` with `.catch(err => logger.error("player", "cleanup_failed", ...))`.

## Acceptance
- Navigate away while in fullscreen: window exits fullscreen before dashboard is visible
- player_unload failures are logged (verifiable by forcing an error in a dev test)

## Required Skills
react, tauri, prexu, anti-fabrication
close_reason: Cleanup now chains unload → fullscreen-exit. player_unload is sync (post prexu-6mf) so by the time fullscreen-exit fires, player_set_fullscreen hits a new fast path (state.is_initialised() == false) that skips the 350ms transition wait + geometry sync. Errors from both invokes are now logged via logger.error instead of silently swallowed. Commit fee7d2e.

title: fix: fullscreen only resizes overlay, video stays small + buttons break
description: After fullscreen toggle, host window z-order gets wrong (ends up in front of webview). Video doesn't resize to fullscreen, and host window blocks mouse events making overlay buttons unclickable. Also isFullscreen state drifts with no event-driven sync from Tauri.
close_reason: Closed

title: fix: resize crash during drag when video is playing
description: During drag-resize, SetWindowPos on the mpv host window causes mpv gpu-next to rebuild D3D11 swapchain. Even at 30Hz throttle, the main thread blocks. Need trailing-edge sync and better z-order management.
close_reason: Closed

title: Native player: fullscreen crashes app + video disappears
description:
Repro: in windowed mode, click the fullscreen button. Controls layer expands as expected but the video region goes blank, then the app crashes shortly after. Hit during Phase 2 acceptance.

Step 2.8 was minimal: useNativePlayer.toggleFullscreen invokes player_set_fullscreen which calls webview_window.set_fullscreen(true). The on_window_event listener catches the resulting Resized event and calls HostWindow::set_geometry to follow.

Likely failure modes:
1. Tauri main's fullscreen transition fires multiple Resized events with intermediate values; sync_geometry SetWindowPos may move the host window to unusable coordinates briefly.
2. Sibling top-level WS_POPUP windows behave oddly when sized to span the full virtual desktop.
3. mpv's gpu-next render context may be invalidated by a rapid resize during fullscreen entry — that could be the crash.
4. Z-order: Tauri main going fullscreen may steal HWND_TOPMOST and our z-order anchor breaks.

Investigation:
- Reproduce, then check Windows Event Viewer / dev log for the crash trace.
- Consider blocking sync_geometry during the fullscreen transition (debounce or detect FS state).
- Consider a dedicated fullscreen path in Rust that explicitly co-positions host+main rather than relying on event-driven sync.

Blocks Phase 4 (mini-player builds on the same window-pair manipulation). Acceptance: F-key toggles fullscreen cleanly with video staying visible and no crash; ESC exits cleanly.

## Required Skills
rust,react,prexu,tauri,anti-fabrication
close_reason: Verified working in dev environment. Fixed across ~20 commits since 2026-04-15, including atomic FS transition (99e785c), main-thread HostWindow ownership (f5bc7bc), SW_SHOWNA focus-steal fix (bac240e), and immediate host geometry sync on FS toggle (99bc012). User confirms fullscreen/exit-fullscreen works cleanly with no crash.

title: Dashboard fetch fails with 'signal is aborted without reason'
description:
On fresh app launch (Plex WebSocket connects fine), the home dashboard renders an error overlay with 'signal is aborted without reason' and a Retry button. The page is otherwise blank.

Caught during Phase 1 native-player smoke testing on 2026-04-15. The error is unrelated to libmpv work — predates this branch (and reproduces immediately on launch before any player code runs).

Symptoms:
- Dashboard data fetch aborts with no actionable reason
- AbortController fires for some reason (timeout? component unmount race?)
- Plex notifications WebSocket succeeds, so server is reachable

Investigation plan:
1. Open devtools network tab on launch, find the aborted fetch
2. Check src/pages/Dashboard.tsx and the hooks it uses (useDashboardData? useContinueWatching?) for AbortController setup
3. Likely culprits: a useEffect cleanup aborting the fetch on a re-render, or an aggressive timeout. Recent player-hook memoization fixes (commit 74ce859) may have shifted re-render timing.
4. Reproduce with a Vitest test if possible.

Acceptance: Dashboard loads its data on first launch without showing the error overlay.

## Required Skills
react,prexu,anti-fabrication
close_reason: Resolved on feature/native-player. timedFetch now aborts with a TimeoutError carrying url + elapsed ms (was 'signal is aborted without reason'); auto-retries once on timeout; default REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS bumped 15s -> 30s. Dashboard refactored to per-section { loading, errors } with independent fetches and a Retry button per row. App splash dismisses on 2-of-3 quorum settle with 20s hard cap, so a slow /library/onDeck no longer strands the user on the splash.

title: Step 6.1 — release.yml: install + cache libmpv on Windows runner
description:
Add job step that downloads mpv-dev-x86_64-v3-*.7z from shinchiro (or vendored mirror), extracts to C:/libmpv/64, generates mpv.lib from libmpv-2.dll exports (dumpbin + lib /def), exports MPV_SOURCE before tauri-action. Cache extracted artifacts by hash so subsequent runs skip download.

## Required Skills
github,prexu

title: Step 6.2 — verify NSIS installer ships libmpv-2.dll
description:
After 6.1 lands, draft a release tag. Inspect resulting NSIS installer with 7z (or install on a clean VM): confirm libmpv-2.dll lands next to Prexu.exe in Program Files/Prexu.

## Required Skills
prexu

title: Step 6.3 — smoke-test installed build plays HEVC
description:
Install draft build, sign in to Plex, play known HEVC Main10 file. Capture log showing mpv hwdec engagement. Promote release if green.

## Required Skills

title: Native player Phase 6 — release/CI for bundled libmpv
description:
After Phase 2 ships and a Windows build is shippable, wire CI so release tags produce installers with libmpv bundled. Without this, Phase 2-4 work is useless to end users.

## Required Skills

title: Step 2.9 — acceptance test: real HEVC Main10 Direct Play
description:
Manual smoke test on Windows 11: pick a known HEVC 10-bit movie from a Plex library, hit play, verify time-pos updates, no slowdown, hwdec engaged (check mpv log for 'Using hardware decoding (d3d11va)' or similar). Document outcome in docs/native-player-status.md.

## Required Skills
prexu,documentation
close_reason: Manual acceptance pass on 2026-04-15. HEVC episode plays at native framerate, video visible behind transparent webview, audio sync correct, seek/scrub responsive, back-button cleanup OK. Three real bugs caught + fixed during acceptance: 4cef687 (error surfacing), 7c83041 (load_url before volume), 6d74a0a (transparent route bg). Open polish: middle-of-screen UI artifact, slow continue-watching prompt — both filed separately.

title: Step 2.8 — fullscreen coordination
description:
Replace document.requestFullscreen with a Tauri command that sets both windows to fullscreen-on-monitor. Exit symmetric. Keep ESC + F-key shortcuts working.

## Required Skills
rust,tauri,prexu
close_reason: Implemented in feature/native-player. cargo check + tsc clean. Visual fullscreen smoke test rolls into step 2.9 acceptance.

title: Step 2.7 — show/hide native window on player route enter/exit
description:
On Player route mount: invoke a player_attach command that sizes + shows the host window. On unmount/navigation: player_detach / player_unload. Avoids the host window peeking through behind library/dashboard pages.

## Required Skills

title: Step 2.6 — refactor usePlayer to delegate native vs HTML5 by platform
description:
Detect platform via @tauri-apps/plugin-os (or capability flag). On Windows return useNativePlayer's result; otherwise return existing HTML5 path. Keep UsePlayerResult shape stable so all consumers (PlayerControls, watch-together hooks, post-play screen) compile unchanged.

## Required Skills

title: Step 2.5 — useNativePlayer hook replacing <video> bindings
description:
New src/hooks/player/useNativePlayer.ts: subscribes to player://* tauri events (time-pos, duration, paused, buffering, eof, ready, error), exposes the same UsePlayerResult-shaped API so existing controls keep working. Action methods invoke player_play / player_pause / player_seek / etc. Memoize aggressively (we burned this before in usePlayer.ts).

## Required Skills
react,prexu,tauri-commands
close_reason: Implemented in feature/native-player. tsc clean. Wiring into usePlayer comes in step 2.6.

title: Step 2.4 — window-group sync (move/resize/minimize/restore/z-order)
description:
Listen to Tauri main-window events (resized, moved, focused, minimized) and propagate to HostWindow via SetWindowPos. Z-order: mpv window stays directly under Tauri main window. Handle DPI changes. Consider WM_DPICHANGED. Also handle: Tauri window close -> destroy mpv host window first.

## Required Skills

close_reason: Implemented in feature/native-player. cargo check clean. Geometry sync, DPI, and shutdown handled. Visual smoke test deferred to step 2.7 (route-aware show/hide) when the host actually becomes visible.

title: Step 2.3 — Tauri main window transparent + always-on-top over mpv
description:
Update tauri.conf.json windows[0]: transparent: true, decorations decision (likely keep, but client-side region masking). Verify Tauri v2 transparent window works under WebView2 on Windows 11. CSP / background must allow click-through over the video region (may need decorations: false + custom titlebar).

## Required Skills

title: Step 2.2 — hand HWND to mpv, configure --wid + gpu-next
description:
On PlayerState::ensure_init: spawn HostWindow, then call mpv.set_property("wid", hwnd_as_i64) BEFORE mpv.command("loadfile", ...). vo stays "gpu-next". Verify libmpv accepts wid as int64 on Windows (mpv docs: --wid takes the window handle as an integer). On destroy, drop HostWindow after mpv terminate.

## Required Skills

title: Step 2.1 — add windows crate, scaffold native window manager
description:
Add windows = { version = "0.x", features = ["Win32_Foundation", "Win32_UI_WindowsAndMessaging", "Win32_Graphics_Gdi"] } to src-tauri/Cargo.toml. Verify version against crates.io. Create src-tauri/src/player/host_window.rs with a HostWindow struct that owns an HWND and exposes create()/destroy()/set_geometry()/set_visible(). Stub bodies are fine; no mpv wiring yet.

## Required Skills
rust,tauri
close_reason: Implemented in feature/native-player. cargo check clean. Surface ready for steps 2.2 (mpv wid handoff), 2.4 (geometry sync), 2.7 (visibility).

title: Native player Phase 2 — two-window composition + swap <video>
description:
Replace HTML5 <video> + hls.js with the libmpv2-backed PlayerState built in Phase 1, on Windows only. macOS/Linux keep the HTML5 path until Phase 5 research concludes.

Approach: transparent Tauri main window on top + sibling native HWND child window hosting mpv via vo=gpu-next + --wid. Synchronised by a Rust window-group manager.

Acceptance: HEVC Main10 file Direct Plays at native framerate on Windows.

Team:
- lead (opus): orchestration + steps 2.4 + 2.5

title: Change default directPlayPreference from 'never' to 'auto'
description: Default was set to 'never' in commit cdc9fd6 to force HLS transcoding during the HTML5 player era. Now that the native (libmpv) path can handle any codec and the HTML5 path guards with canDirectPlay() codec whitelist, 'auto' is the sensible default. Users currently on 'never' due to this default never benefit from the Direct Play fix in prexu-fmd.7.
close_reason: Closed

title: Native player: continue-watching prompt is slow to appear
description:
Clicking play on a partially-watched item shows blank for 1-2 seconds before the 'Continue from xx:xx / Start from beginning' prompt appears. Caught during Phase 2 acceptance.

Likely causes:
- getItemMetadata Plex API call latency (network-bound; hard to fix without caching)
- useNativePlayer's setIsLoading sequence holds back the prompt component until metadata resolves
- The prompt is a separate hook/component that waits on player metadata that didn't have to wait before

Investigation: profile the path between play-click and prompt-render. Add timing logs.

Acceptance: prompt appears within ~200ms of click for items with cached metadata, or shows a loading indicator immediately if data is still being fetched.

react,prexu
close_reason: Resolved on feature/native-player. 3uj: mpv default osd-level=1/osd-bar=yes drew a horizontal progress bar in the middle of the video on every seek; with the transparent webview it showed through alongside our React seek bar. Set osd-level=0 and osd-bar=no on mpv init. dcj: usePlayAction now trusts cached item.viewOffset (always present on onDeck/recents) and shows ResumePopover instantly with no network round trip. Slow path (no cached offset) shows a small loading popover at click point for immediate feedback.

title: Native player: misrendered UI element appears in middle of player route
description:
After Phase 2 transparency fixes (commit 6d74a0a), a horizontal bar / outlined rectangle appears in the middle of the player route, visible over the video. Most likely a piece of player chrome (SeekBar, buffered indicator, or controls overlay) that previously blended into a colored background and now reads weirdly with body transparent.

Diagnosis: right-click the artifact in Tauri devtools and identify the element. Likely either:
- SeekBar buffered fill rendering with buffered=0 (we stubbed that in useNativePlayer; step 3.10 lights it up)
- An overlay (PostPlay placeholder, KeyboardShortcuts) with previously-blending bg
- The hidden <video> element somehow still occupying space

Acceptance: artifact gone or styled to look intentional on both native + html5 paths.

Code path verified end-to-end.

Now the same handleEnded callback subscribes to player://eof via Tauri's listen() on the native path, and to the videoRef 'ended' event on the HTML5 path. title: Step 3.5 — Watch Together drives native player
description:
useRemotePlayback currently calls togglePlay/seek on UsePlayerResult. Verify that path still works after Phase 2 hook abstraction. Test sync drift detection works against player://time-pos events. Relay server unchanged.

## Required Skills
react,prexu
close_reason: Two latent bugs surfaced and fixed: (1) useRemotePlayback.ts:65 read player.videoRef.current and bailed if null, dropping every remote_play/remote_pause/remote_seek silently on the native path. Now routed through player.seek + player.togglePlay, which work on both backends; isPlaying is checked before togglePlay so play/pause set the desired state rather than blindly toggle. (2) useSyncDriftDetection.ts:36 had the same pattern for drift correction — also routed through player.seek. playerRef indirection lets long-lived listeners read the latest player state without re-binding watchSync subs on every render. Drift detection works against player.currentTime which is already populated from player://time-pos events on the native path, so no other changes were needed. tsc clean, vitest 1163/1163.

The original convertFileSrc + asset protocol path remains in the HTML5 path (usePlayer.ts:252-255) which is correct — it's only used by non-Windows platforms. file:// prefix not added because mpv handles raw Windows paths fine and avoids URL-encoding edge cases (spaces, special chars in download paths).

title: Step 3.1 — audio track switching to player_set_audio_track
description:
Update useStreamSelection to dispatch player_set_audio_track(id) for native path. Plex stream IDs may not match mpv aid track index — likely need mapping via mpv track-list events.

## Required Skills
react,prexu
close_reason: Replaced direct Plex stream ID → mpv aid/sid pass-through (broken: Plex IDs are global Library IDs like 234567, mpv expects 1-indexed track positions). New mapping uses positional lookup against the categorized audio/subtitle arrays (categorizeStreams preserves file order, so audioTracks[i] ↔ mpv aid=i+1). Action callbacks read tracks via audioTracksRef/subtitleTracksRef so they don't rebind on track-list changes. initPlayback now also dispatches the initial track selection (after load_url, before await for ready) so preferredAudioLanguage and 'subtitles off' both take effect on file load instead of waiting for a user click. Subtitle 'off' explicitly dispatches sid=null since mpv's default would show the first sub track. Note: positional mapping assumes Plex's Stream array order matches the file's track order; track-list-event-driven mapping (more robust for files where Plex injects ordering) deferred — open follow-up if mismatches surface in the wild. tsc clean.

title: Step 3.2 — subtitle track switching to player_set_sub_track
description:

All 10 child steps closed: 3.1 audio track switching (Plex ID → mpv aid positional mapping), 3.2 subtitle track switching (same), 3.3 subtitle styling (player_apply_sub_style libass command + initial+change wiring), 3.4 audio enhancements UI (player_set_af_chain + player_set_audio_delay_ms dispatch), 3.5 Watch Together drives native player (fixed videoRef.current bailouts in useRemotePlayback + useSyncDriftDetection), 3.6 offline downloads via mpv (already done — raw Windows path), 3.7 relax Direct Play (already closed), 3.8 resume position via start_offset_ms (already done in initPlayback), 3.9 PostPlay listens to player://eof (subscribed via Tauri listen on native path), 3.10 buffered range reporting (REPLY_BUFFERED on demuxer-cache-time, throttled to 2 Hz). Vitest 1163/1163, tsc + cargo check clean. Phase 4 (mini-player) is now unblocked.

title: Investigate tao 'RedrawEventsCleared without MainEventsCleared' warnings on startup
description:
Dev log at 2026-04-20 01:44:25 shows two tao warnings emitted before the first user interaction:

  [tao::platform_impl::platform::event_loop::runner][WARN] NewEvents emitted without explicit RedrawEventsCleared
  [tao::platform_impl::platform::event_loop::runner][WARN] RedrawEventsCleared emitted without explicit MainEventsCleared

Possibly from our custom main-thread dispatcher (run_on_main_thread) for HostWindow creation/destruction, or from WebView2 init. Not a functional bug — playback works fine — but the warnings indicate an event-loop protocol mismatch that may hide a latent sync issue.

Investigate: reproduce with/without player usage, correlate with host_window.rs dispatch sites, check if upgrading tao/tauri clears them.

title: Debounce/throttle player seek invocations during scrub drag
description: Log review from 2026-04-20 01:44:49-55 showed 5 seek commands fired within ~4 seconds, four of them to the identical target (199.77s). Every pointer movement on the seek bar invokes player_seek directly. Each seek triggers a full Plex transcode segment fetch + mpv seek + decode restart, so rapid drags stack up redundant work.\n\nFix: throttle seek to ~100-200ms during pointer-down, or fire only on pointer-up (with a preview display during drag). Preserve keyboard arrow-key behavior (each key press = one seek, no drag to debounce).

title: Native player Phase 5 — research cross-platform feasibility
description:
Single research issue. Decide whether and how to extend native player to macOS + Linux. Document findings in docs/native-player-status.md. Cover: macOS NSView + libmpv on Apple Silicon (VideoToolbox), Linux X11 vs Wayland surface handoff, libmpv distribution per platform, CI cost for matrix builds. Output: ADR-style decision in docs/.

## Required Skills
rust,tauri,documentation,anti-fabrication

value: SUPERSEDED 2026-04-20: fullscreen now works end-to-end. See docs/native-player-status.md §'Architecture notes & hard-won lessons' for the full constraint set. Key: HostWindow MUST be main-thread-owned (run_on_main_thread dispatch in ensure_init); ShowWindow uses SW_SHOWNA (SW_SHOW activates despite WS_EX_NOACTIVATE when thread pumps msgs); destroy() joins event pump before dropping Inner so mpv_terminate_destroy runs sync and silences audio; body-bg swap MUST be useLayoutEffect (useEffect cleanup is post-paint, window is transparent, Discord leaks through otherwise); handleBack awaits player_set_fullscreen(false) before navigate(-1). prexu-dsh (mpv D3D11 swapchain doesn't follow host on fullscreen) remains open — do NOT resize mpv's GW_CHILD from host WndProc, that hang-deadlocks main.

On Prexu's feature/native-player branch: do NOT merge to main mid-stream. Push the branch for backup, but only merge once ALL the work for the next release is complete. The user batches release work on a single long-running branch and merges once at the end.

On Prexu, the user batches unrelated fixes onto the active long-running feature branch when they're all shipping in the same release. Don't insist on per-fix branches — confirm the release intent and batch.
